NKJV.1: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I do not want you to be ignorant:
NKJV.2: You know that you were Gentiles, carried away to these dumb idols, however you were led.
NKJV.3: Therefore I make known to you that no one speaking by the Spirit of God calls Jesus accursed, and no one can say that Jesus is Lord except by the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.4: There are diversities of gifts, but the same Spirit.
NKJV.5: There are differences of ministries, but the same Lord.
NKJV.6: And there are diversities of activities, but it is the same God who works all in all.
NKJV.7: But the manifestation of the Spirit is given to each one for the profit of all:
NKJV.8: for to one is given the word of wisdom through the Spirit, to another the word of knowledge through the same Spirit,
NKJV.9: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healings by the same Spirit,
NKJV.10: to another the working of miracles, to another prophecy, to another discerning of spirits, to another different kinds of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues.
NKJV.11: But one and the same Spirit works all these things, distributing to each one individually as He wills.
NKJV.12: For as the body is one and has many members, but all the members of that one body, being many, are one body, so also is Christ.
NKJV.13: For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body— whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free—and have all been made to drink into one Spirit.
NKJV.14: For in fact the body is not one member but many.
NKJV.15: If the foot should say, “Because I am not a hand, I am not of the body,” is it therefore not of the body?
NKJV.16: And if the ear should say, “Because I am not an eye, I am not of the body,” is it therefore not of the body?
NKJV.17: If the whole body were an eye, where would be the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where would be the smelling?
NKJV.18: But now God has set the members, each one of them, in the body just as He pleased.
NKJV.19: And if they were all one member, where would the body be?
NKJV.20: But now indeed there are many members, yet one body.
NKJV.21: And the eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you”; nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
NKJV.22: No, much rather, those members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary.
NKJV.23: And those members of the body which we think to be less honorable, on these we bestow greater honor; and our unpresentable parts have greater modesty,
NKJV.24: but our presentable parts have no need. But God composed the body, having given greater honor to that part which lacks it,
NKJV.25: that there should be no schism in the body, but that the members should have the same care for one another.
NKJV.26: And if one member suffers, all the members suffer with it; or if one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
NKJV.27: Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.
NKJV.28: And God has appointed these in the church: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, administrations, varieties of tongues.
NKJV.29: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all workers of miracles?
NKJV.30: Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
NKJV.31: But earnestly desire the best gifts. And yet I show you a more excellent way.

ESV.1: Now concerning  spiritual gifts,  brothers,  I do not want you to be uninformed.
ESV.2: You know that when you were pagans you were led astray to mute idols, however you were led.
ESV.3: Therefore I want you to understand that no one speaking in the Spirit of God ever says “Jesus is accursed!” and no one can say “Jesus is Lord” except in the Holy Spirit.
ESV.4: Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit;
ESV.5: and there are varieties of service, but the same Lord;
ESV.6: and there are varieties of activities, but it is the same God who empowers them all in everyone.
ESV.7: To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
ESV.8: For to one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom, and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit,
ESV.9: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit,
ESV.10: to another the working of miracles, to another prophecy, to another the ability to distinguish between spirits, to another various kinds of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues.
ESV.11: All these are empowered by one and the same Spirit, who apportions to each one individually as he wills.
ESV.12: For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so it is with Christ.
ESV.13: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body — Jews or Greeks, slaves  or free — and all were made to drink of one Spirit.
ESV.14: For the body does not consist of one member but of many.
ESV.15: If the foot should say, “Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
ESV.16: And if the ear should say, “Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
ESV.17: If the whole body were an eye, where would be the sense of hearing? If the whole body were an ear, where would be the sense of smell?
ESV.18: But as it is, God arranged the members in the body, each one of them, as he chose.
ESV.19: If all were a single member, where would the body be?
ESV.20: As it is, there are many parts,  yet one body.
ESV.21: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
ESV.22: On the contrary, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
ESV.23: and on those parts of the body that we think less honorable we bestow the greater honor, and our unpresentable parts are treated with greater modesty,
ESV.24: which our more presentable parts do not require. But God has so composed the body, giving greater honor to the part that lacked it,
ESV.25: that there may be no division in the body, but that the members may have the same care for one another.
ESV.26: If one member suffers, all suffer together; if one member is honored, all rejoice together.
ESV.27: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.
ESV.28: And God has appointed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healing, helping, administrating, and various kinds of tongues.
ESV.29: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
ESV.30: Do all possess gifts of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
ESV.31: But earnestly desire the higher gifts. And I will show you a still more excellent way.

NASB.1: Now concerning spiritual [gifts], brethren, I do not want you to be unaware.
NASB.2: You know that when you were pagans, [you were] led astray to the mute idols, however you were led.
NASB.3: Therefore I make known to you that no one speaking by the Spirit of God says, 'Jesus is accursed'; and no one can say, 'Jesus is Lord,' except by the Holy Spirit.
NASB.4: Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit.
NASB.5: And there are varieties of ministries, and the same Lord.
NASB.6: There are varieties of effects, but the same God who works all things in all [persons].
NASB.7: But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
NASB.8: For to one is given the word of wisdom through the Spirit, and to another the word of knowledge according to the same Spirit;
NASB.9: to another faith by the same Spirit, and to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit,
NASB.10: and to another the effecting of miracles, and to another prophecy, and to another the distinguishing of spirits, to another [various] kinds of tongues, and to another the interpretation of tongues.
NASB.11: But one and the same Spirit works all these things, distributing to each one individually just as He wills.
NASB.12: For even as the body is one and [yet] has many members, and all the members of the body, though they are many, are one body, so also is Christ.
NASB.13: For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free, and we were all made to drink of one Spirit.
NASB.14: For the body is not one member, but many.
NASB.15: If the foot says, 'Because I am not a hand, I am not [a part] of the body,' it is not for this reason any the less [a part] of the body.
NASB.16: And if the ear says, 'Because I am not an eye, I am not [a part] of the body,' it is not for this reason any the less [a part] of the body.
NASB.17: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?
NASB.18: But now God has placed the members, each one of them, in the body, just as He desired.
NASB.19: If they were all one member, where would the body be?
NASB.20: But now there are many members, but one body.
NASB.21: And the eye cannot say to the hand, 'I have no need of you'; or again the head to the feet, 'I have no need of you.'
NASB.22: On the contrary, it is much truer that the members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary;
NASB.23: and those [members] of the body which we deem less honorable, on these we bestow more abundant honor, and our less presentable members become much more presentable,
NASB.24: whereas our more presentable members have no need [of it]. But God has [so] composed the body, giving more abundant honor to that [member] which lacked,
NASB.25: so that there may be no division in the body, but [that] the members may have the same care for one another.
NASB.26: And if one member suffers, all the members suffer with it; if [one] member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
NASB.27: Now you are Christ's body, and individually members of it.
NASB.28: And God has appointed in the church, first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, administrations, [various] kinds of tongues.
NASB.29: All are not apostles, are they? All are not prophets, are they? All are not teachers, are they? All are not [workers of] miracles, are they?
NASB.30: All do not have gifts of healings, do they? All do not speak with tongues, do they? All do not interpret, do they?
NASB.31: But earnestly desire the greater gifts. And I show you a still more excellent way.

NLT.1: Now, dear brothers and sisters, regarding your question about the special abilities the Spirit gives us. I don’t want you to misunderstand this.
NLT.2: You know that when you were still pagans, you were led astray and swept along in worshiping speechless idols.
NLT.3: So I want you to know that no one speaking by the Spirit of God will curse Jesus, and no one can say Jesus is Lord, except by the Holy Spirit.
NLT.4: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but the same Spirit is the source of them all.
NLT.5: There are different kinds of service, but we serve the same Lord.
NLT.6: God works in different ways, but it is the same God who does the work in all of us.
NLT.7: A spiritual gift is given to each of us so we can help each other.
NLT.8: To one person the Spirit gives the ability to give wise advice ; to another the same Spirit gives a message of special knowledge.
NLT.9: The same Spirit gives great faith to another, and to someone else the one Spirit gives the gift of healing.
NLT.10: He gives one person the power to perform miracles, and another the ability to prophesy. He gives someone else the ability to discern whether a message is from the Spirit of God or from another spirit. Still another person is given the ability to speak in unknown languages, while another is given the ability to interpret what is being said.
NLT.11: It is the one and only Spirit who distributes all these gifts. He alone decides which gift each person should have.
NLT.12: The human body has many parts, but the many parts make up one whole body. So it is with the body of Christ.
NLT.13: Some of us are Jews, some are Gentiles, some are slaves, and some are free. But we have all been baptized into one body by one Spirit, and we all share the same Spirit.
NLT.14: Yes, the body has many different parts, not just one part.
NLT.15: If the foot says, “I am not a part of the body because I am not a hand,” that does not make it any less a part of the body.
NLT.16: And if the ear says, “I am not part of the body because I am not an eye,” would that make it any less a part of the body?
NLT.17: If the whole body were an eye, how would you hear? Or if your whole body were an ear, how would you smell anything?
NLT.18: But our bodies have many parts, and God has put each part just where he wants it.
NLT.19: How strange a body would be if it had only one part!
NLT.20: Yes, there are many parts, but only one body.
NLT.21: The eye can never say to the hand, “I don’t need you.” The head can’t say to the feet, “I don’t need you.”
NLT.22: In fact, some parts of the body that seem weakest and least important are actually the most necessary.
NLT.23: And the parts we regard as less honorable are those we clothe with the greatest care. So we carefully protect those parts that should not be seen,
NLT.24: while the more honorable parts do not require this special care. So God has put the body together such that extra honor and care are given to those parts that have less dignity.
NLT.25: This makes for harmony among the members, so that all the members care for each other.
NLT.26: If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it, and if one part is honored, all the parts are glad.
NLT.27: All of you together are Christ’s body, and each of you is a part of it.
NLT.28:
Here are some of the parts God has appointed for the church: first are apostles,
second are prophets,
third are teachers,
then those who do miracles,
those who have the gift of healing,
those who can help others,
those who have the gift of leadership,
those who speak in unknown languages.
NLT.29: Are we all apostles? Are we all prophets? Are we all teachers? Do we all have the power to do miracles?
NLT.30: Do we all have the gift of healing? Do we all have the ability to speak in unknown languages? Do we all have the ability to interpret unknown languages? Of course not!
NLT.31: So you should earnestly desire the most helpful gifts. But now let me show you a way of life that is best of all.

WEB.1: Now concerning spiritual things, brothers, I don’t want you to be ignorant.
WEB.2: You know that when you were heathen,  you were led away to those mute idols, however you might be led.
WEB.3: Therefore I make known to you that no man speaking by God’s Spirit says, “Jesus is accursed.” No one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” but by the Holy Spirit.
WEB.4: Now there are various kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit.
WEB.5: There are various kinds of service, and the same Lord.
WEB.6: There are various kinds of workings, but the same God, who works all things in all.
WEB.7: But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the profit of all.
WEB.8: For to one is given through the Spirit the word of wisdom, and to another the word of knowledge, according to the same Spirit;
WEB.9: to another faith, by the same Spirit; and to another gifts of healings, by the same Spirit;
WEB.10: and to another workings of miracles; and to another prophecy; and to another discerning of spirits; to another different kinds of languages; and to another the interpretation of languages.
WEB.11: But the one and the same Spirit produces all of these, distributing to each one separately as he desires.
WEB.12: For as the body is one, and has many members, and all the members of the body, being many, are one body; so also is Christ.
WEB.13: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body, whether Jews or Greeks, whether bond or free; and were all given to drink into one Spirit.
WEB.14: For the body is not one member, but many.
WEB.15: If the foot would say, “Because I’m not the hand, I’m not part of the body,” it is not therefore not part of the body.
WEB.16: If the ear would say, “Because I’m not the eye, I’m not part of the body,” it’s not therefore not part of the body.
WEB.17: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole were hearing, where would the smelling be?
WEB.18: But now God has set the members, each one of them, in the body, just as he desired.
WEB.19: If they were all one member, where would the body be?
WEB.20: But now they are many members, but one body.
WEB.21: The eye can’t tell the hand, “I have no need for you,” or again the head to the feet, “I have no need for you.”
WEB.22: No, much rather, those members of the body which seem to be weaker are necessary.
WEB.23: Those parts of the body which we think to be less honorable, on those we bestow more abundant honor; and our unpresentable parts have more abundant propriety;
WEB.24: whereas our presentable parts have no such need. But God composed the body together, giving more abundant honor to the inferior part,
WEB.25: that there should be no division in the body, but that the members should have the same care for one another.
WEB.26: When one member suffers, all the members suffer with it. Or when one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
WEB.27: Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.
WEB.28: God has set some in the assembly: first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracle workers, then gifts of healings, helps, governments, and various kinds of languages.
WEB.29: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all miracle workers?
WEB.30: Do all have gifts of healings? Do all speak with various languages? Do all interpret?
WEB.31: But earnestly desire the best gifts. Moreover, I show a most excellent way to you.

CSB17.1: Now concerning spiritual gifts: <sup>[1]</sup> brothers and sisters, I do not want you to be unaware.
CSB17.2: You know that when you were pagans, you used to be enticed and led astray by mute <sup>ⓜ</sup> idols. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.3: Therefore I want you to know that no one speaking by the Spirit of God says, “Jesus is cursed,” and no one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” except by the Holy Spirit.
CSB17.4: Now there are different gifts, <sup>ⓐ</sup> but the same Spirit.
CSB17.5: There are different ministries, but the same Lord.
CSB17.6: And there are different activities, but the same God produces each gift in each person.
CSB17.7: A manifestation of the Spirit is given to each person <sup>ⓑ</sup> for the common good:
CSB17.8: to one <sup>ⓒ</sup> is given a message of wisdom <sup>ⓓ</sup> through the Spirit, to another, a message of knowledge by the same Spirit,
CSB17.9: to another, faith by the same Spirit, to another, gifts of healing <sup>ⓔ</sup> by the one Spirit,
CSB17.10: to another, the performing of miracles, <sup>ⓕ</sup> to another, prophecy, <sup>ⓖ</sup> to another, distinguishing between spirits, <sup>ⓗ</sup> to another, different kinds of tongues, <sup>[2]</sup> to another, interpretation of tongues.
CSB17.11: One and the same Spirit is active in all these, distributing to each person as he wills.
CSB17.12: For just as the body is one <sup>ⓘ</sup> and has many parts, and all the parts of that body, though many, are one body — so also is Christ. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.13: For we were all baptized by <sup>[3]</sup> one Spirit into one body — whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free — and we were all given one Spirit to drink. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.14: Indeed, the body is not one part but many.
CSB17.15: If the foot should say, “Because I’m not a hand, I don’t belong to the body,” it is not for that reason any less a part of the body.
CSB17.16: And if the ear should say, “Because I’m not an eye, I don’t belong to the body,” it is not for that reason any less a part of the body.
CSB17.17: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole body were an ear, where would the sense of smell be?
CSB17.18: But as it is, God has arranged each one of the parts in the body just as he wanted. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.19: And if they were all the same part, where would the body be?
CSB17.20: As it is, there are many parts, but one body.
CSB17.21: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I don’t need you!” Or again, the head can’t say to the feet, “I don’t need you!”
CSB17.22: On the contrary, those parts of the body that are weaker are indispensable.
CSB17.23: And those parts of the body that we consider less honorable, <sup>ⓜ</sup> we clothe these with greater honor, and our unrespectable parts are treated with greater respect,
CSB17.24: which our respectable parts do not need. Instead, God has put the body together, giving greater honor to the less honorable,
CSB17.25: so that there would be no division <sup>ⓝ</sup> in the body, but that the members would have the same concern for each other.
CSB17.26: So if one member suffers, all the members suffer with it; if one member is honored, all the members rejoice with it.
CSB17.27: Now you are the body of Christ, <sup>ⓐ</sup> and individual members of it.
CSB17.28: And God has appointed these in the church: <sup>ⓑ</sup> first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, <sup>ⓒ</sup> next miracles, <sup>ⓓ</sup> then gifts of healing, <sup>ⓔ</sup> helping, administrating, various kinds of tongues. <sup>[4]</sup>
CSB17.29: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all do miracles?
CSB17.30: Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak in other tongues? Do all interpret? <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.31: But desire <sup>ⓖ</sup> the greater gifts. And I will show you an even better way.

AMP.1: Now about the spiritual gifts [the special endowments given by the Holy Spirit], brothers and sisters, I do not want you to be uninformed.
AMP.2: You know that when you were pagans, you were led off after speechless idols; however you were led off [whether by impulse or habit].
AMP.3: Therefore I want you to know that no one speaking by the [power and influence of the] Spirit of God can say, "Jesus be cursed," and no one can say, "Jesus is [my] Lord," except by [the power and influence of] the Holy Spirit.
AMP.4: Now there are [distinctive] varieties of spiritual gifts [special abilities given by the grace and extraordinary power of the Holy Spirit operating in believers], but it is the same Spirit [who grants them and empowers believers].
AMP.5: And there are [distinctive] varieties of ministries and service, but it is the same Lord [who is served].
AMP.6: And there are [distinctive] ways of working [to accomplish things], but it is the same God who produces all things in all believers [inspiring, energizing, and empowering them].
AMP.7: But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit [the spiritual illumination and the enabling of the Holy Spirit] for the common good.
AMP.8: To one is given through the [Holy] Spirit [the power to speak] the message of wisdom, and to another [the power to express] the word of knowledge and understanding according to the same Spirit;
AMP.9: to another [wonder-working] faith [is given] by the same [Holy] Spirit, and to another the [extraordinary] gifts of healings by the one Spirit;
AMP.10: and to another the working of miracles, and to another prophecy [foretelling the future, speaking a new message from God to the people], and to another discernment of spirits [the ability to distinguish sound, godly doctrine from the deceptive doctrine of man-made religions and cults], to another various kinds of [unknown] tongues, and to another interpretation of tongues.
AMP.11: All these things [the gifts, the achievements, the abilities, the empowering] are brought about by one and the same [Holy] Spirit, distributing to each one individually just as He chooses.
AMP.12: For just as the body is one and yet has many parts, and all the parts, though many, form [only] one body, so it is with Christ.
AMP.13: For by one [Holy] Spirit we were all baptized into one body, [spiritually transformed—united together] whether Jews or Greeks (Gentiles), slaves or free, and we were all made to drink of one [Holy] Spirit [since the same Holy Spirit fills each life].
AMP.14: For the [human] body does not consist of one part, but of many [limbs and organs].
AMP.15: If the foot says, "Because I am not a hand, I am not a part of the body," is it not on the contrary still a part of the body?
AMP.16: If the ear says, "Because I am not an eye, I am not a part of the body," is it not on the contrary still a part of the body?
AMP.17: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole [body] were an ear, where would the sense of smell be?
AMP.18: But now [as things really are], God has placed and arranged the parts in the body, each one of them, just as He willed and saw fit [with the best balance of function].
AMP.19: If they all were a single organ, where would [the rest of] the body be?
AMP.20: But now [as things really are] there are many parts [different limbs and organs], but a single body.
AMP.21: The eye cannot say to the hand, "I have no need of you," nor again the head to the feet, "I have no need of you."
AMP.22: But quite the contrary, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker are [absolutely] necessary;
AMP.23: and as for those parts of the body which we consider less honorable, these we treat with greater honor; and our less presentable parts are treated with greater modesty,
AMP.24: while our more presentable parts do not require it. But God has combined the [whole] body, giving greater honor to that part which lacks it,
AMP.25: so that there would be no division or discord in the body [that is, lack of adaptation of the parts to each other], but that the parts may have the same concern for one another.
AMP.26: And if one member suffers, all the parts share the suffering; if one member is honored, all rejoice with it.
AMP.27: Now you [collectively] are Christ's body, and individually [you are] members of it [each with his own special purpose and function].
AMP.28: So God has appointed and placed in the church [for His own use]: first apostles [chosen by Christ], second prophets [those who foretell the future, those who speak a new message from God to the people], third teachers, then those who work miracles, then those with the gifts of healings, the helpers, the administrators, and speakers in various kinds of [unknown] tongues.
AMP.29: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Are all workers of miracles?
AMP.30: Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
AMP.31:
But earnestly desire and strive for the greater gifts [if acquiring them is going to be your goal].
And yet I will show you a still more excellent way [one of the choicest graces and the highest of them all: unselfish love].

MSG.1: What I want to talk about now is the various ways God's Spirit gets worked into our lives. This is complex and often misunderstood, but I want you to be informed and knowledgeable.
MSG.2: Remember how you were when you didn't know God, led from one phony god to another, never knowing what you were doing, just doing it because everybody else did it? It's different in this life. God wants us to use our intelligence, to seek to understand as well as we can.
MSG.3: For instance, by using your heads, you know perfectly well that the Spirit of God would never prompt anyone to say "Jesus be damned!" Nor would anyone be inclined to say "Jesus is Master!" without the insight of the Holy Spirit.
MSG.4: God's various gifts are handed out everywhere; but they all originate in God's Spirit.
MSG.5: God's various ministries are carried out everywhere; but they all originate in God's Spirit.
MSG.6: God's various expressions of power are in action everywhere; but God himself is behind it all.
MSG.7: Each person is given something to do that shows who God is: Everyone gets in on it, everyone benefits. All kinds of things are handed out by the Spirit, and to all kinds of people!
MSG.8: The variety is wonderful: wise counsel clear understanding
MSG.10: miraculous acts proclamation distinguishing between spirits tongues interpretation of tongues.
MSG.11: All these gifts have a common origin, but are handed out one by one by the one Spirit of God. He decides who gets what, and when.
MSG.12: You can easily enough see how this kind of thing works by looking no further than your own body. Your body has many parts—limbs, organs, cells—but no matter how many parts you can name, you're still one body. It's exactly the same with Christ.
MSG.13: By means of his one Spirit, we all said good-bye to our partial and piecemeal lives. We each used to independently call our own shots, but then we entered into a large and integrated life in which he has the final say in everything. (This is what we proclaimed in word and action when we were baptized.) Each of us is now a part of his resurrection body, refreshed and sustained at one fountain—his Spirit—where we all come to drink. The old labels we once used to identify ourselves—labels like Jew or Greek, slave or free—are no longer useful. We need something larger, more comprehensive.
MSG.14: I want you to think about how all this makes you more significant, not less. A body isn't just a single part blown up into something huge. It's all the different-but-similar parts arranged and functioning together.
MSG.15: If Foot said, "I'm not elegant like Hand, embellished with rings; I guess I don't belong to this body," would that make it so?
MSG.16: If Ear said, "I'm not beautiful like Eye, limpid and expressive; I don't deserve a place on the head," would you want to remove it from the body?
MSG.17: If the body was all eye, how could it hear? If all ear, how could it smell?
MSG.18: As it is, we see that God has carefully placed each part of the body right where he wanted it.
MSG.19: But I also want you to think about how this keeps your significance from getting blown up into self-importance. For no matter how significant you are, it is only because of what you are a part of. An enormous eye or a gigantic hand wouldn't be a body, but a monster.
MSG.20: What we have is one body with many parts, each its proper size and in its proper place. No part is important on its own.
MSG.21: Can you imagine Eye telling Hand, "Get lost; I don't need you"? Or, Head telling Foot, "You're fired; your job has been phased out"?
MSG.22: As a matter of fact, in practice it works the other way—the "lower" the part, the more basic, and therefore necessary. You can live without an eye, for instance, but not without a stomach.
MSG.23: When it's a part of your own body you are concerned with, it makes no difference whether the part is visible or clothed, higher or lower. You give it dignity and honor just as it is, without comparisons.
MSG.24: If anything, you have more concern for the lower parts than the higher. If you had to choose, wouldn't you prefer good digestion to full-bodied hair?
MSG.25: The way God designed our bodies is a model for understanding our lives together as a church: every part dependent on every other part, the parts we mention and the parts we don't,
MSG.26: the parts we see and the parts we don't. If one part hurts, every other part is involved in the hurt, and in the healing. If one part flourishes, every other part enters into the exuberance.
MSG.27: You are Christ's body—that's who you are! You must never forget this. Only as you accept your part of that body does your "part" mean anything.
MSG.28: You're familiar with some of the parts that God has formed in his church, which is his "body": apostles prophets teachers miracle workers healers helpers organizers those who pray in tongues.
MSG.29: But it's obvious by now, isn't it, that Christ's church is a complete Body and not a gigantic, unidimensional Part? It's not all Apostle, not all Prophet, not all Miracle Worker,
MSG.30: not all Healer, not all Prayer in Tongues, not all Interpreter of Tongues.
MSG.31: And yet some of you keep competing for so-called "important" parts. But now I want to lay out a far better way for you.

NIV.1:
Spiritual Gifts
Now about spiritual gifts, brothers, I do not want you to be ignorant.
NIV.2: You know that when you were pagans, somehow or other you were influenced and led astray to mute idols.
NIV.3: Therefore I tell you that no one who is speaking by the Spirit of God says, “Jesus be cursed,” and no one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” except by the Holy Spirit.
NIV.4: There are different kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit.
NIV.5: There are different kinds of service, but the same Lord.
NIV.6: There are different kinds of working, but the same God works all of them in all men.
NIV.7: Now to each one the manifestation of the Spirit is given for the common good.
NIV.8: To one there is given through the Spirit the message of wisdom, to another the message of knowledge by means of the same Spirit,
NIV.9: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healing by that one Spirit,
NIV.10: to another miraculous powers, to another prophecy, to another distinguishing between spirits, to another speaking in different kinds of tongues, and to still another the interpretation of tongues.
NIV.11: All these are the work of one and the same Spirit, and he gives them to each one, just as he determines.
NIV.12:
One Body, Many Parts
The body is a unit, though it is made up of many parts; and though all its parts are many, they form one body. So it is with Christ.
NIV.13: For we were all baptized by one Spirit into one body—whether Jews or Greeks, slave or free—and we were all given the one Spirit to drink.
NIV.14: Now the body is not made up of one part but of many.
NIV.15: If the foot should say, “Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,” it would not for that reason cease to be part of the body.
NIV.16: And if the ear should say, “Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,” it would not for that reason cease to be part of the body.
NIV.17: If the whole body were an eye, where would the sense of hearing be? If the whole body were an ear, where would the sense of smell be?
NIV.18: But in fact God has arranged the parts in the body, every one of them, just as he wanted them to be.
NIV.19: If they were all one part, where would the body be?
NIV.20: As it is, there are many parts, but one body.
NIV.21: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I don’t need you!” And the head cannot say to the feet, “I don’t need you!”
NIV.22: On the contrary, those parts of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
NIV.23: and the parts that we think are less honorable we treat with special honor. And the parts that are unpresentable are treated with special modesty,
NIV.24: while our presentable parts need no special treatment. But God has combined the members of the body and has given greater honor to the parts that lacked it,
NIV.25: so that there should be no division in the body, but that its parts should have equal concern for each other.
NIV.26: If one part suffers, every part suffers with it; if one part is honored, every part rejoices with it.
NIV.27: Now you are the body of Christ, and each one of you is a part of it.
NIV.28: And in the church God has appointed first of all apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then workers of miracles, also those having gifts of healing, those able to help others, those with gifts of administration, and those speaking in different kinds of tongues.
NIV.29: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
NIV.30: Do all have gifts of healing? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?
NIV.31:
But eagerly desire the greater gifts.
Love
And now I will show you the most excellent way.

NRSVCE.1: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brothers and sisters, I do not want you to be uninformed.
NRSVCE.2: You know that when you were pagans, you were enticed and led astray to idols that could not speak.
NRSVCE.3: Therefore I want you to understand that no one speaking by the Spirit of God ever says “Let Jesus be cursed!” and no one can say “Jesus is Lord” except by the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.4: Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit;
NRSVCE.5: and there are varieties of services, but the same Lord;
NRSVCE.6: and there are varieties of activities, but it is the same God who activates all of them in everyone.
NRSVCE.7: To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
NRSVCE.8: To one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom, and to another the utterance of knowledge according to the same Spirit,
NRSVCE.9: to another faith by the same Spirit, to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit,
NRSVCE.10: to another the working of miracles, to another prophecy, to another the discernment of spirits, to another various kinds of tongues, to another the interpretation of tongues.
NRSVCE.11: All these are activated by one and the same Spirit, who allots to each one individually just as the Spirit chooses.
NRSVCE.12: For just as the body is one and has many members, and all the members of the body, though many, are one body, so it is with Christ.
NRSVCE.13: For in the one Spirit we were all baptized into one body — Jews or Greeks, slaves or free — and we were all made to drink of one Spirit.
NRSVCE.14: Indeed, the body does not consist of one member but of many.
NRSVCE.15: If the foot would say, “Because I am not a hand, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
NRSVCE.16: And if the ear would say, “Because I am not an eye, I do not belong to the body,” that would not make it any less a part of the body.
NRSVCE.17: If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be? If the whole body were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?
NRSVCE.18: But as it is, God arranged the members in the body, each one of them, as he chose.
NRSVCE.19: If all were a single member, where would the body be?
NRSVCE.20: As it is, there are many members, yet one body.
NRSVCE.21: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I have no need of you,” nor again the head to the feet, “I have no need of you.”
NRSVCE.22: On the contrary, the members of the body that seem to be weaker are indispensable,
NRSVCE.23: and those members of the body that we think less honorable we clothe with greater honor, and our less respectable members are treated with greater respect;
NRSVCE.24: whereas our more respectable members do not need this. But God has so arranged the body, giving the greater honor to the inferior member,
NRSVCE.25: that there may be no dissension within the body, but the members may have the same care for one another.
NRSVCE.26: If one member suffers, all suffer together with it; if one member is honored, all rejoice together with it.
NRSVCE.27: Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.
NRSVCE.28: And God has appointed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers; then deeds of power, then gifts of healing, forms of assistance, forms of leadership, various kinds of tongues.
NRSVCE.29: Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
NRSVCE.30: Do all possess gifts of healing? Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?
NRSVCE.31: But strive for the greater gifts. And I will show you a still more excellent way.

NET.1: With regard to spiritual gifts, brothers and sisters, I do not want you to be uninformed.
NET.2: You know that when you were pagans you were often led astray by speechless idols, however you were led.
NET.3: So I want you to understand that no one speaking by the Spirit of God says, “Jesus is cursed,” and no one can say, “Jesus is Lord,” except by the Holy Spirit.
NET.4: Now there are different gifts, but the same Spirit.
NET.5: And there are different ministries, but the same Lord.
NET.6: And there are different results, but the same God who produces all of them in everyone.
NET.7: To each person the manifestation of the Spirit is given for the benefit of all.
NET.8: For one person is given through the Spirit the message of wisdom, and another the message of knowledge according to the same Spirit,
NET.9: to another faith by the same Spirit, and to another gifts of healing by the one Spirit,
NET.10: to another performance of miracles, to another prophecy, and to another discernment of spirits, to another different kinds of tongues, and to another the interpretation of tongues.
NET.11: It is one and the same Spirit, distributing as he decides to each person, who produces all these things.
NET.12: For just as the body is one and yet has many members, and all the members of the body — though many — are one body, so too is Christ.
NET.13: For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body. Whether Jews or Greeks or slaves or free, we were all made to drink of the one Spirit.
NET.14: For in fact the body is not a single member, but many.
NET.15: If the foot says, “Since I am not a hand, I am not part of the body,” it does not lose its membership in the body because of that.
NET.16: And if the ear says, “Since I am not an eye, I am not part of the body,” it does not lose its membership in the body because of that.
NET.17: If the whole body were an eye, what part would do the hearing? If the whole were an ear, what part would exercise the sense of smell?
NET.18: But as a matter of fact, God has placed each of the members in the body just as he decided.
NET.19: If they were all the same member, where would the body be?
NET.20: So now there are many members, but one body.
NET.21: The eye cannot say to the hand, “I do not need you,” nor in turn can the head say to the foot, “I do not need you.”
NET.22: On the contrary, those members that seem to be weaker are essential,
NET.23: and those members we consider less honorable we clothe with greater honor, and our unpresentable members are clothed with dignity,
NET.24: but our presentable members do not need this. Instead, God has blended together the body, giving greater honor to the lesser member,
NET.25: so that there may be no division in the body, but the members may have mutual concern for one another.
NET.26: If one member suffers, everyone suffers with it. If a member is honored, all rejoice with it.
NET.27: Now you are Christ’s body, and each of you is a member of it.
NET.28: And God has placed in the church first apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then miracles, gifts of healing, helps, gifts of leadership, different kinds of tongues.
NET.29: Not all are apostles, are they? Not all are prophets, are they? Not all are teachers, are they? Not all perform miracles, do they?
NET.30: Not all have gifts of healing, do they? Not all speak in tongues, do they? Not all interpret, do they?
NET.31: But you should be eager for the greater gifts. And now I will show you a way that is beyond comparison.
